questions.question:
The `frontend-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q01` was changed during a failed release. The rollout is paused and the active template references a bad image tag, so the application is not becoming available.

Tasks:
- Resume the rollout for `frontend-api` if it is paused.
- Set the container `frontend` image to `nginx:1.27`.
- Keep exactly `3` replicas and make the rollout complete successfully.

verification.description: Deployment uses the fixed `nginx:1.27` image.
Deployment has 3 replicas and is not paused.
Deployment rollout is available.

questions.question:
The `orders-web` Deployment in namespace `cka-q02` has a broken readiness probe and unsafe rolling-update values. Pods start, but they are never marked ready and the rollout cannot provide the expected availability.

Tasks:
- Fix the readiness probe to check HTTP path `/` on port `80`.
- Use a safe rolling update policy with `maxUnavailable: 0` and `maxSurge: 1`.
- Ensure `orders-web` has exactly `4` available replicas.

verification.description: Readiness probe uses HTTP `/` on port 80.
RollingUpdate strategy prevents unavailable pods during rollout.
Deployment has four available replicas.

questions.question:
The `ledger-db` StatefulSet in namespace `cka-q03` was recreated with the wrong `serviceName`. Stable DNS names such as `ledger-db-0.ledger-db-hl.cka-q03.svc.cluster.local` do not resolve because the required headless Service is missing and the StatefulSet points at the wrong service.

Tasks:
- Create a headless Service named `ledger-db-hl` selecting `app=ledger-db`.
- Recreate/fix the StatefulSet so `.spec.serviceName` is `ledger-db-hl`.
- Keep `2` ready replicas with stable names `ledger-db-0` and `ledger-db-1`.

verification.description: Headless Service exists and selects the StatefulSet pods.
StatefulSet serviceName points to the headless Service.
Both StatefulSet pods are ready with stable names.

questions.question:
The `metrics-store` StatefulSet in namespace `cka-q04` was deployed with ephemeral storage. The application requires one persistent PVC per replica named from a `volumeClaimTemplates` entry called `data`, mounted at `/usr/share/nginx/html`.

Tasks:
- Recreate/fix `metrics-store` with a `volumeClaimTemplates` entry named `data`.
- Use `ReadWriteOnce`, request `128Mi`, and keep `storageClassName: standard` unless your cluster requires another default class.
- Run `2` ready replicas with each pod mounting the `data` claim at `/usr/share/nginx/html`.

verification.description: StatefulSet defines the required volumeClaimTemplates entry.
Container mounts the `data` claim at the required path.
Per-pod PVCs exist and StatefulSet is ready.

questions.question:
The `node-log-agent` DaemonSet in namespace `cka-q05` must run on every cluster node. It currently misses the control-plane toleration, so it skips tainted control-plane nodes.

Tasks:
- Add a toleration for `node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule` using operator `Exists`.
- Keep the DaemonSet selector/template label `app=node-log-agent`.
- Ensure the desired number scheduled equals the total node count and all scheduled pods are ready.

verification.description: DaemonSet tolerates the control-plane NoSchedule taint.
DaemonSet desired count matches total node count.
All desired DaemonSet pods are ready.

questions.question:
The `packet-capture` DaemonSet in namespace `cka-q06` should run only on nodes labeled `q06.capture=true`. The DaemonSet already has that nodeSelector, but no schedulable node has the required label, so no agent pod starts.

Tasks:
- Label exactly one schedulable worker node with `q06.capture=true`.
- Keep the DaemonSet nodeSelector as `q06.capture=true`.
- Ensure one `packet-capture` DaemonSet pod is ready on the labeled node.

verification.description: At least one node is labeled for packet capture.
DaemonSet uses the required nodeSelector.
Exactly one DaemonSet pod is scheduled and ready.

questions.question:
A batch manifest at `/tmp/exam/q07/checksum-job.yaml` was rejected because the Job template uses `restartPolicy: Always`. Create/fix the `checksum-job` in namespace `cka-q07` so it completes successfully.

Tasks:
- Fix the manifest or create the Job with `restartPolicy: OnFailure` or `Never`.
- Use container image `busybox:1.36` and run `sh -c "echo checksum-ok"`.
- Ensure the Job completes successfully exactly once.

verification.description: Job exists with a valid restartPolicy.
Job uses expected image and command.
Job completes successfully.

questions.question:
The `db-cleanup` CronJob in namespace `cka-q08` was left suspended after maintenance and uses the wrong concurrency policy and schedule. It should run every five minutes without overlapping runs.

Tasks:
- Set schedule to `*/5 * * * *`.
- Set `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` and `suspend: false`.
- Set `timeZone: Asia/Colombo` and keep the Job template restart policy valid.

verification.description: CronJob has the required schedule and timezone.
CronJob is unsuspended and prevents overlap.
CronJob template uses a valid restartPolicy.

questions.question:
The `slow-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q09` takes about 25 seconds to create its health file. Its liveness probe checks too early and restarts the container before startup completes.

Tasks:
- Add a `startupProbe` that checks `/tmp/healthy` with enough failures to allow slow startup.
- Keep the liveness probe as an exec check for `/tmp/healthy` but make it safe after startup.
- Ensure the Deployment reaches one available replica.

verification.description: Deployment has a startupProbe for `/tmp/healthy` with adequate threshold.
Liveness probe checks the same health file safely.
slow-api becomes available.

questions.question:
The `catalog-web` Service in namespace `cka-q10` has no usable ready endpoints because the Deployment readiness probe checks a non-existent path. Fix the workload so traffic can reach ready pods.

Tasks:
- Change the readiness probe HTTP path to `/` on port `80`.
- Keep the Service selector `app=catalog-web` matching the pod template label.
- Ensure at least two ready endpoints exist for Service `catalog-web`.

verification.description: Readiness probe path and port are correct.
Service selector matches Deployment pod labels.
Service has at least two ready endpoints.

questions.question:
The `config-consumer` Deployment in namespace `cka-q11` is stuck in `CreateContainerConfigError`. It references a ConfigMap key with the wrong case, so the required `APP_MODE` environment variable is not injected.

Tasks:
- Fix the environment variable reference so `APP_MODE` comes from ConfigMap `app-settings` key `APP_MODE`.
- Keep the container image `busybox:1.36` and command running.
- Ensure the pod becomes ready and `APP_MODE` resolves to `production`.

verification.description: Deployment references the correct ConfigMap key.
Deployment is available.
Running pod has APP_MODE=production.

questions.question:
The `payment-worker` Deployment in namespace `cka-q12` cannot start because its `DB_PASSWORD` environment variable references the wrong Secret key. The Secret exists in the correct namespace with key `password`.

Tasks:
- Fix the Secret key reference from `pwd` to `password`.
- Keep the Secret named `db-credentials` in namespace `cka-q12`.
- Ensure the pod is ready and `DB_PASSWORD` resolves to `s3cr3t`.

verification.description: Deployment references Secret `db-credentials` key `password`.
Deployment is available.
Running pod receives DB_PASSWORD=s3cr3t.

questions.question:
The standalone Pod `pinned-cache` in namespace `cka-q13` is hard-pinned to `ghost-node` using `spec.nodeName`, bypassing the scheduler. It must be recreated so the scheduler can place it normally.

Tasks:
- Recreate `pinned-cache` without `spec.nodeName`.
- Keep image `nginx:1.27` and label `app=pinned-cache`.
- Ensure the Pod is Running on a real node.

verification.description: Pod does not use hard `nodeName` pinning.
Pod keeps required image and label.
Pod is Running and Ready.

questions.question:
The `reporting-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q14` uses `nodeSelector: q14.disk=ssd`, but no node has that label. The workload must run only on a node labeled for SSD capacity.

Tasks:
- Label one schedulable node with `q14.disk=ssd`.
- Keep the Deployment nodeSelector exactly `q14.disk=ssd`.
- Ensure both replicas are ready and scheduled only on nodes with that label.

verification.description: A node has label `q14.disk=ssd`.
Deployment keeps required nodeSelector.
Pods are ready on labeled nodes only.

questions.question:
The `analytics-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q15` uses required node affinity for a GPU label that does not exist in the cluster. The team only wanted GPU nodes to be preferred, not required.

Tasks:
- Remove `requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution` from node affinity.
- Add/keep a preferred node affinity rule for `q15.accelerator=gpu` with weight `80` or higher.
- Ensure two replicas become available on any schedulable nodes.

verification.description: Hard required node affinity is removed.
Preferred node affinity for GPU label exists.
analytics-api has two available replicas.

questions.question:
The `reserved-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q16` must run on a reserved node labeled `q16.pool=reserved`. The setup taints that node with reserved workload taints, but the pod template has no matching tolerations, so replicas remain Pending.

Tasks:
- Keep `nodeSelector: q16.pool=reserved`.
- Add tolerations for `q16.pool=reserved:NoSchedule` and `q16.soft=reserved:PreferNoSchedule`.
- Also add a bounded `NoExecute` toleration for `q16.evict=reserved` with `tolerationSeconds: 300`, then ensure the Deployment is available.

verification.description: Deployment targets the reserved pool by nodeSelector.
Required NoSchedule and PreferNoSchedule tolerations exist.
NoExecute toleration is bounded and Deployment is available.

questions.question:
The `ha-web` Deployment in namespace `cka-q17` requires two replicas not to co-locate on the same node. The anti-affinity rule uses a custom rack topology where all nodes have the same value, so the second replica cannot schedule. Use node hostname topology instead.

Tasks:
- Set required podAntiAffinity topologyKey to `kubernetes.io/hostname`.
- Keep the anti-affinity label selector matching `app=ha-web`.
- Ensure two replicas are ready on different nodes.

verification.description: Anti-affinity topologyKey is hostname.
Anti-affinity selector matches `app=ha-web`.
Two replicas are ready on different nodes.

questions.question:
The `pay-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q18` is supposed to spread four replicas across hostnames with `maxSkew: 1`, but its topology spread label selector points at the wrong app label. The constraint is not protecting placement.

Tasks:
- Set the topologySpreadConstraints labelSelector to match `app=pay-api`.
- Use `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname`, `maxSkew: 1`, and `whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule`.
- Ensure four replicas are available and spread across at least two nodes.

verification.description: Topology spread constraint uses hostname, maxSkew 1, DoNotSchedule.
Spread constraint selector matches `app=pay-api`.
Four replicas are available and on at least two nodes.

questions.question:
The `quota-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q19` exists, but its ReplicaSet cannot create pods because container requests exceed the namespace ResourceQuota. Reduce requests/limits to fit the quota while keeping explicit resource settings.

Tasks:
- Set container requests to `cpu: 100m` and `memory: 64Mi`.
- Set limits to `cpu: 200m` and `memory: 128Mi`.
- Ensure two replicas become available without deleting the ResourceQuota `compute-quota`.

verification.description: Deployment has the required CPU and memory requests.
Deployment has required limits and ResourceQuota still exists.
quota-api has two available replicas.

questions.question:
The `critical-api` Deployment in namespace `cka-q20` references PriorityClass `business-critical`, but the PriorityClass does not exist. The workload must use a high scheduling priority with normal preemption enabled.

Tasks:
- Create PriorityClass `business-critical` with value at least `100000`.
- Set `preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority` and `globalDefault: false`.
- Ensure Deployment `critical-api` uses this PriorityClass and has one available replica.

verification.description: PriorityClass exists with high value and non-default setting.
PriorityClass enables preemption and Deployment uses it.
critical-api is available.
